codes.title: 100 Days At Sea Codes
codes.description: All active 100 Days At Sea codes for free Pearls, with clear redemption steps, expiry notes, and where new codes drop during Roblox events and updates.
codes.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Codes - Free Pearls & Active Rewards
codes.metaDescription: Redeem the latest active 100 Days At Sea codes for free Pearls. Step-by-step redemption, expiry notes, and where to find new codes, kept up to date.
codes.whatIs: What Are 100 Days At Sea Codes?
codes.whatIsDescription: 100 Days At Sea codes are free reward strings released by Stranded Devs through Roblox events and game updates. Entering an active code in the in-game Codes menu grants Pearls, the premium currency used to unlock classes and open companion chests for your survival run.

whySteps.step1Description: Active codes drop Pearls that unlock starter classes like Survivor or Medic, giving your early days more room for mistakes.
whySteps.step2Description: Saved Pearls open companion chests sooner, adding passive perks like loot pickup, melee damage, or food gathering to your run.
whySteps.step3Description: Codes are claimed only through the in-game Codes menu, never through external login forms, scripts, or browser extensions.

guide.title: 100 Days At Sea Guide
guide.description: Complete 100 Days At Sea guides covering beginner tips, full walkthroughs, the survival loop, objectives, endings, and solo strategies for reaching Day 100.
guide.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Guide - Walkthrough, Tips & Day 100
guide.metaDescription: Master 100 Days At Sea with beginner tips, full walkthroughs, survival objectives, endings, solo strategies, and how long it takes to beat the game.
guide.whatIs: What Is the 100 Days At Sea Guide?
guide.whatIsDescription: The 100 Days At Sea guide breaks down the full survival loop in the Bermuda Triangle: gather resources with your harpoon, build a raft base with structures and defenses, explore mysterious islands, find weapons, and push toward surviving 100 days.

whySteps.step1Description: Start with the right order—gather first, build second, explore third—so early days stay stable instead of chaotic.
whySteps.step2Description: Build for survival before decoration: bonfire levels, food support, and defenses keep the run alive through raids.
whySteps.step3Description: Walkthroughs and objectives map out milestones, endings, and solo strategies so the long run toward Day 100 stays on track.

classes.title: 100 Days At Sea Classes
classes.description: Every 100 Days At Sea class ranked by Pearl cost, starter tool, buffs, and best survival role, with a full tier list for beginner, combat, and Day 100 runs.
classes.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Classes - Tier List & Best Class
classes.metaDescription: Compare every 100 Days At Sea class by Pearl cost, starter tool, and buff. Find the best class for beginner safety, boss combat, and Day 100 survival.
classes.whatIs: What Are 100 Days At Sea Classes?
classes.whatIsDescription: Classes in 100 Days At Sea are loadouts unlocked with Pearls. Each class grants a passive advantage—faster regeneration, extra gun damage, better boat handling, or movement speed—so the right pick changes how safe, fast, or combat-ready a run feels.

whySteps.step1Description: Starter classes like Survivor add regeneration, oxygen, and hunger control, giving new players more room for mistakes.
whySteps.step2Description: Damage classes like Sharpshooter, Pirate, and Raider scale gun output and ammo flow for boss fights and island raids.
whySteps.step3Description: Matching your class to your companion and weapon build turns Pearl spending into a clear long-term advantage.

crafting.title: 100 Days At Sea Crafting
crafting.description: 100 Days At Sea crafting and base building guides covering the raft, container shelter, outpost, medkit, fish recipes, and bonfire progression for safer runs.
crafting.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Crafting - Raft, Buildings & Recipes
crafting.metaDescription: Craft and build in 100 Days At Sea: raft expansion, container shelter, outpost, medkit, fish recipes, and bonfire upgrades for safer survival runs.
crafting.whatIs: What Is Crafting in 100 Days At Sea?
crafting.whatIsDescription: Crafting in 100 Days At Sea turns gathered resources into the structures, defenses, food, and tools that keep your raft base running. From the starter raft to the outpost, medkit, and cooked meals, every build decision affects how long you survive.

whySteps.step1Description: Build raft structures and defenses early so the base survives raids and night pressure while you gather and explore.
whySteps.step2Description: Recipes like fish filet, fish sandwich, and chowder keep hunger and health under control on longer island routes.
whySteps.step3Description: Upgrading the bonfire and shelter turns base building into visible milestones that lead toward Day 100.

revive.title: 100 Days At Sea Revive
revive.description: How to revive teammates and save sailors in 100 Days At Sea, covering the defibrillator, multiplayer revive rules, and survivor rescue strategies for co-op runs.
revive.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Revive - Save Teammates & Sailors
revive.metaDescription: Learn how to revive in 100 Days At Sea: defibrillator use, multiplayer revive mechanics, and how to save the sailor and survivors in co-op Day 100 runs.
revive.whatIs: What Is Revive in 100 Days At Sea?
revive.whatIsDescription: Revive in 100 Days At Sea covers the mechanics that bring downed crewmates back into the run and the rescue goals that save sailors and survivors. The defibrillator is the core tool, while multiplayer revive rules decide how a co-op crew recovers from a wipe.

whySteps.step1Description: Knowing the defibrillator and multiplayer revive rules stops a single down from snowballing into a failed run.
whySteps.step2Description: Saving the sailor and rescued survivors unlocks key completion milestones tied to Day 100 and challenge badges.
whySteps.step3Description: A crew that can revive pushes harder island routes and boss fights without ending the run on one mistake.

locations.title: 100 Days At Sea Locations
locations.description: Every 100 Days At Sea location and landmark guide: oil rig, cargo ship, cruise ship, crewmate yacht, ghost ship, volcano, red key, and dinghy fuel routes.
locations.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Locations - Ships, Islands & Volcano
locations.metaDescription: Explore 100 Days At Sea locations: oil rig, cargo ship, cruise ship, ghost ship, volcano, red key, and dinghy fuel routes with loot and danger notes.
locations.whatIs: What Are 100 Days At Sea Locations?
locations.whatIsDescription: Locations in 100 Days At Sea are the ships, islands, and landmarks you sail to for better loot and progression. From the oil rig and cargo ship to the cruise ship, ghost ship, and volcano, each route carries its own rewards, threats, and key items like the red key and dinghy fuel.

whySteps.step1Description: Ships and landmarks like the oil rig and cargo ship drop stronger gear than open-water gathering.
whySteps.step2Description: Routes tied to the red key and dinghy fuel unlock new areas and keep island exploration moving forward.
whySteps.step3Description: Volcano, ghost ship, and cruise ship routes connect to region bosses and rare exploration badges.

seeds.title: 100 Days At Sea Seeds
seeds.description: The best 100 Days At Sea seeds and map seeds for custom spawns, ice biomes, hard runs, and resource-rich starts. Find the right seed for your survival run.
seeds.metaTitle: 100 Days At Sea Seeds - Best Map & Custom Seeds
seeds.metaDescription: Discover the best 100 Days At Sea seeds: map seeds, ice seeds, hard seeds, and custom seeds for resource-rich starts and challenge runs.
seeds.whatIs: What Are 100 Days At Sea Seeds?
seeds.whatIsDescription: Seeds in 100 Days At Sea control how the map, islands, biomes, and resource spawns generate for a run. A custom seed can drop you into an ice biome, a resource-rich start, or a hard survival challenge, letting you shape the run before Day 1.

whySteps.step1Description: A chosen map seed sets nearby islands, resources, and biomes so you can plan your opening days instead of relying on luck.
whySteps.step2Description: Ice seeds and biome-specific seeds route you straight into the regions and bosses you want to practice.
whySteps.step3Description: Hard seeds create tougher survival conditions for experienced players chasing challenge runs and badges.

codes.title: 100 Days At Sea Codes
codes.intro: 100 Days At Sea uses Roblox reward codes to grant extra resources before a survival run. Redeeming an active code early gives you free Pearls, and the codes menu is the only safe place to claim them as you push toward 100 days at sea.

activeCode.bestUse: Save the Pearls for an early class unlock or a companion chest instead of spending them right away.

redeemSteps: Launch 100 Days At Sea on Roblox.
Select the CODES button in the top-left area of the game screen.
Press Submit and confirm that the Pearls were added to your balance.

sourceCheck.title: Where to Check New Codes
points: Roblox game page: watch the experience title, description, and event wording during updates.
Stranded Devs community: use the developer community page for update context and event news.
In-game Codes menu: redeem only through the game UI, never through external login forms or scripts.

safety.title: Code Rules That Matter
points: Codes are one-use per Roblox account.
Codes can expire after events or patches, so redeem them quickly.
Avoid script executors, browser extensions, or pages asking for a Roblox password.

beginnerGuide.title: How to Play 100 Days At Sea
beginnerGuide.intro: 100 Days At Sea is an open-world Roblox survival game where exploration and base building drive every run. The clean beginner path is simple: gather first, build second, explore third, and fight only when your raft and tools are ready for 100 days at sea.

steps.title: Start the Ocean Mission
steps.goal: Load into the Roblox experience and focus on surviving, not rushing.
steps.whatToDo: Learn the controls, stay close to your raft, and treat Day 100 as the long-term goal.
steps.whyItMatters: The game is built around surviving 100 days while uncovering the Bermuda Triangle mystery.

steps.title: Use the Harpoon for Early Resources
steps.goal: Pull useful items from the sea before leaving your safe area.
steps.whatToDo: Grab floating resources or hook them with your Harpoon whenever you sail or wait near the raft.
steps.whyItMatters: Harpoon gathering is one of the core actions listed on the official Roblox page.

steps.title: Build a Compact Raft Base
steps.goal: Turn the starter platform into a defensible base.
steps.whatToDo: Build raft structures first, then add defenses before expanding too wide.
steps.whyItMatters: Base building is the main survival layer, and over-expanding before defense makes the raft harder to protect.

steps.title: Explore Islands After Setup
steps.goal: Use islands for better resources, not as a place to get caught out early.
steps.whatToDo: Visit mysterious islands after you have basic supplies, storage habits, and a return route to the raft.
steps.whyItMatters: Island exploration is central to progression, but it becomes safer once the raft can support longer trips.

steps.title: Prepare Weapons and Defenses
steps.goal: Handle hostile encounters before they snowball into a failed run.
steps.whatToDo: Find weapons, keep defenses repaired, and avoid starting risky fights while low on food or supplies.
steps.whyItMatters: The official game description tells players to find weapons and prepare defenses before facing foes.

steps.title: Repeat the Survival Loop
steps.whatToDo: Gather resources, upgrade the raft, explore farther islands, return with loot, and prepare for the next danger spike.
steps.whyItMatters: 100 Days At Sea rewards steady setup more than reckless exploration.

classesTierList.title: 100 Days At Sea Classes Tier List
classesTierList.intro: Classes in 100 Days At Sea are loadouts unlocked with Pearls. Each class gives a passive advantage, so the right pick changes how safe, fast, or combat-ready a run feels.

items.name: Survivor
items.mainBuff: 10% faster regeneration, extra oxygen, and less hunger.
items.bestFor: Beginner survival and solo Day 1-30 safety.
items.note: The strongest first unlock for players who need more room for mistakes.

items.name: Medic
items.mainBuff: Faster health regeneration.
items.bestFor: Co-op support, recovery-focused runs, and safer boss preparation.
items.note: A strong support pick when one player needs to keep the crew alive.

items.name: Sharpshooter
items.mainBuff: Faster reload and 50% more gun damage.
items.note: High value once ammo and ranged weapons become part of the run.

items.name: Pirate
items.mainBuff: Better boat handling and 50% more gun damage.
items.bestFor: All-around combat with stronger sea travel.
items.note: Excellent when you want both damage and better navigation.

items.name: Raider
items.mainBuff: More ammo every five kills, ammo drops from defeated enemies, and 15% faster sprint.
items.bestFor: Island raids and sustained gun fights.
items.note: Expensive, but built for combat loops where ammo flow matters.

items.name: Fire Mage
items.mainBuff: 50% more Magic Damage.
items.bestFor: Late-game magic damage and high-threat encounters.
items.note: A premium damage class for players already past the basics.

items.name: Sailor
items.mainBuff: Faster top speed and better boat handling.
items.bestFor: Island travel and first cheap unlocks.
items.note: Simple, cheap, and useful for players doing a lot of sailing.

items.name: Crewmate
items.mainBuff: 10% more gun damage.
items.note: Good low-cost damage option before expensive gun classes.

items.name: Swordsman
items.mainBuff: 50% more melee damage and 20% extra health.
items.note: Strong if you prefer close combat and can manage risk.

items.name: Cowboy
items.mainBuff: 15% more health and 33% more gun damage.
items.note: A balanced ranged class with extra durability.

items.name: Soldier
items.mainBuff: 20% faster sprinting.
items.note: The starter Assault Rifle gives it practical value beyond the sprint buff.

items.name: Night
items.mainBuff: Knight Armor, 30% damage reduction, and 15% more sprint.
items.note: Great for players who want damage reduction before harder fights.

items.name: Hero
items.mainBuff: 25% more health and 25% less hunger.
items.note: Strong stats, but the cost makes it a later investment.

items.name: Zookeeper
items.mainBuff: Supercharges Companions.
items.note: Gets better after you have strong companion rolls.

items.name: Battle Bunny
items.mainBuff: Egg Grenades from enemies and chests, double jump, and 20% faster sprint.
items.note: A flexible advanced pick with movement value.

items.name: Camper
items.mainBuff: +10% health.
items.note: Useful early, but less impactful than Survivor or Medic.

items.name: Adventurer
items.mainBuff: 15% more health and less hunger.
items.note: Solid utility, but sits behind stronger survival and damage classes.

items.name: Olympian
items.mainBuff: 25% faster sprint and 20% extra health.
items.note: Good comfort pick, weaker if you need starter tools.

items.name: Battle Buku
items.mainBuff: 25% more melee damage and a MukuMuku discount.
items.bestFor: Melee damage with a shop discount angle.
items.note: Useful, but Swordsman has the cleaner melee damage profile.

items.name: Merchant
items.mainBuff: 25% more Doubloons.
items.note: Better for players who already know where spending routes pay off.

items.name: Millionaire
items.mainBuff: Spawn with a Jumbo Luxury Yacht and 100x Doubloons.
items.note: Strong opening comfort, but less direct survival value than combat or healing.

items.name: Treasure Hunter
items.mainBuff: Five more stack slots and 25% more Doubloons.
items.note: Good for efficient players, less important for first clears.

items.name: Ancient Squid
items.mainBuff: 15% more sprint speed.
items.note: Expensive for a sprint-focused buff, but the starter tool can define niche runs.

companionsTierList.title: 100 Days At Sea Companions Tier List
companionsTierList.intro: Companions in 100 Days At Sea come from Pearl chests and add passive perks such as damage boosts, sprint speed, food gathering, material gathering, or hunger reduction. The best rolls either automate survival chores or improve combat.

items.name: Salamander
items.perk: Picks up and delivers nearby loot.

items.name: Swordfish
items.sources: Warrior Chest — 80 Pearls (5%) · Pirate Chest — 120 Pearls (5%)

items.name: Pirate Gunner
items.bestFor: Gun classes, boss pressure, and ranged builds.

items.name: Octopus
items.perk: Brings materials from surroundings every 120 seconds.
items.bestFor: Long runs where passive material income saves time.

items.name: Crow
items.perk: Searches surroundings and brings food every 120 seconds.

items.name: Seagull
items.bestFor: Food support and safer exploration pacing.

items.name: Parrot
items.bestFor: Ranged players who do not roll Pirate Gunner yet.

items.name: Shark
items.sources: Fisherman's Chest — 40 Pearls (13%) · Warrior Chest — 80 Pearls (16%)
items.bestFor: Early melee damage before rare companions.

items.name: Dolphin
items.sources: Fisherman's Chest — 40 Pearls (13%) · Magma Chest — 120 Pearls (30%)
items.bestFor: Exploration, repositioning, and faster island movement.

items.name: Lava Snail
items.bestFor: Longer survival windows when food is tight.

items.name: Crab
items.bestFor: Starter ranged damage before better Pirate Chest rolls.

items.name: Frog
items.bestFor: Early hunger help until stronger rolls replace it.

badgesAndAchievements.title: 100 Days At Sea Badges and Achievement Goals
badgesAndAchievements.subtitle: Track the main badge routes for survival days, bonfire upgrades, combat wins, exploration, rescue goals, and loot challenges.
badgesAndAchievements.intro: 100 Days At Sea badges are built around the full survival loop: start a run, use the harpoon, upgrade the raft base, survive longer day milestones, explore islands, defeat bosses, rescue biome targets, and complete rare run challenges.

items.unlock: Play the game for the first time.
Grab an object with your harpoon.
Cook your first chowder with the cooking pot.
Get a gun from a chest.
Visit all the islands in a single session.
Defeat the boss in the Ice Biome.
Rescue all of the penguins in the Ice Biome.

islandsBossesAndWeapons.title: 100 Days At Sea Islands, Bosses, and Weapon Routes
islandsBossesAndWeapons.subtitle: Use island exploration to move from basic raft survival into boss fights, biome goals, and stronger combat unlocks.
islandsBossesAndWeapons.intro: The official game loop points players from harpoon gathering into raft building, island exploration, weapon finding, and boss combat. The safest progression path is to stabilize the raft first, then route islands and biome bosses around badges and weapon goals.

items.title: Open-Sea Island Route
items.focus: Sail out from the raft to visit mysterious islands, gather better loot, and prepare for longer survival milestones.
items.weaponFocus: Chest weapons and basic combat tools

items.title: Chest Weapon Route
items.focus: Use island and chest runs to improve combat readiness before boss attempts.
items.bossOrThreat: Standard enemies, raids, and higher-risk island fights

items.title: Squid Boss Route
items.focus: Prepare food, healing, weapons, and raft safety before attempting the Squid Boss.
items.weaponFocus: Boss-ready ranged damage and sustained survival supplies

items.title: Ghost Galleon Route
items.focus: Push beyond normal raid survival by defeating the Ghost Galleon multiple times in one run.
items.weaponFocus: Consistent boss damage and enough supplies for repeated fights

items.title: Rival Rig Route
items.focus: Find and defeat all 3 rival rigs in a single session for a rare combat-exploration clear.
items.weaponFocus: Sustained weapons, ammo, and healing between rig fights

items.title: Volcano Region Route
items.focus: Visit the Volcano region, defeat Fire Golems, and take on the Lord of Lava route.
items.bossOrThreat: Fire Golems and Lord of Lava
items.weaponFocus: Magma Staff route and fire-region combat prep

items.title: Ice Biome Route
items.focus: Enter the Ice Biome, rescue penguins, and defeat the Ice Biome boss.
items.weaponFocus: Ice-region survival and boss damage setup

items.title: Day 100 Completion Route
items.focus: Combine island loot, raft upgrades, food, weapons, and boss avoidance or boss clearing into a full day 100 survival run.
items.bossOrThreat: Long-run pressure, raids, hunger, and route mistakes
items.weaponFocus: Chest weapons, boss-capable damage, and enough supplies for late days

raftBaseBuildingGuide.title: 100 Days At Sea Raft Base Building Guide
raftBaseBuildingGuide.subtitle: Build for survival first: resources, space, defenses, food, weapons, and bonfire progress matter more than decoration early.
raftBaseBuildingGuide.intro: Raft building is one of the core survival systems in 100 Days At Sea. A strong early base keeps the run stable while you gather with the harpoon, cook food, find weapons, survive raids, and push toward longer day milestones.

items.title: Hook Resources Before Taking Risks
items.objective: Use the harpoon to pull in early resources and avoid wasting health on unsafe movement.
actions: Grab floating objects with the harpoon.
Keep gathering before leaving the raft for longer routes.
Use early resources on survival tools and raft progress first.

items.title: Expand the Raft Around Function
items.objective: Create enough room for movement, storage habits, cooking, and defense placement.
actions: Build a stable working area before chasing a large decorative raft.
Keep key survival structures easy to reach.
Leave space to move during raids and night pressure.

items.title: Push Bonfire Levels Early
items.objective: Use bonfire progress as the clearest raft-base milestone path.
actions: Upgrade bonfire levels as the run develops.
Use Bonfire 1 through Bonfire 9 as visible progress checks.
Treat Bonfire Master as a fast-upgrade challenge for experienced players.

items.title: Add Food Support Before Long Trips
items.objective: Make the raft support healing and hunger before committing to bigger island routes.
actions: Cook chowder with the cooking pot.
Stock food before longer exploration loops.
Return to the raft to reset supplies instead of overextending.

items.title: Build With Night Defense in Mind
items.objective: Place structures and defenses so the raft can survive attacks while still giving you room to fight.
actions: Avoid blocking movement around key raft structures.
Use weapons found from chests to strengthen defense before boss routes.

items.title: Prepare the Raft Before Boss Routes
items.objective: Treat the raft as the launch point for Squid Boss, Ice Biome, Volcano, and day 100 attempts.
actions: Bring food, healing, and stronger weapons before leaving for boss-focused regions.
Do not start rare boss or biome objectives from a weak raft.
Use survival day badges to judge whether the base is ready for harder routes.
items.badgeSignal: Arms of Victory, King Of The Snow, Rescued!

items.label: Core Survival Loop
items.details: The official page describes the loop as grabbing resources or hooking them with the harpoon, building a raft base with structures and defenses, exploring mysterious islands, finding weapons, and taking on fearsome foes.
items.playerFocus: Best for beginner guide, how to play, harpoon, raft, islands, weapons, and bosses searches.

faq.title: Your questions about
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about codes, classes, raft survival, and the 100-day run in 100 Days At Sea.

questions.question: What is 100 Days At Sea?
questions.answer: 100 Days At Sea is an open-world Roblox survival game by Stranded Devs. You gather resources with a harpoon, build a raft base, explore mysterious islands, find weapons, and push toward surviving 100 days in the Bermuda Triangle.

questions.question: Where can I play 100 Days At Sea?
questions.answer: 100 Days At Sea is free to play on Roblox across PC, mobile, and console platforms that support the Roblox app. Launch it directly from the official Roblox game page.

questions.question: How do I redeem 100 Days At Sea codes?
questions.answer: Open 100 Days At Sea, tap the CODES button in the top-left of the screen, enter an active code like 20Pearls, and press Submit to claim rewards such as free Pearls.

questions.question: What are Pearls used for in 100 Days At Sea?
questions.answer: In 100 Days At Sea, Pearls unlock classes and open companion chests. Saving them for an early class or a strong companion roll speeds up your survival runs toward 100 days at sea.

questions.question: Is 100 Days At Sea multiplayer?
questions.answer: 100 Days At Sea supports up to 27 players per server, so you can survive the ocean, build rafts, raid islands, and fight bosses together with friends.

questions.question: What is the goal of 100 Days At Sea?
questions.answer: The core goal of 100 Days At Sea is to survive 100 days at sea while upgrading your raft, exploring islands, defeating bosses, and uncovering the Bermuda Triangle mystery.

cta.title: Ready to Survive 100 Days At Sea?
cta.description: Dive into 100 Days At Sea on Roblox, gather resources with your harpoon, build your raft, and redeem codes for free pearls.
